description: A secret stored as an unprotected variable is exposed to feature branches and fork merge-request pipelines.
body: Failure mode: a credential is added as a CI/CD variable without the Protected flag, so it is injected into pipelines on every branch and into fork merge-request pipelines, where the MR author's code runs with the secret in scope. Detection signals: a secret variable not marked Protected; a feature-branch or fork MR pipeline able to read it; the value usable from attacker-controlled job scripts. Do not store secrets as unprotected variables. Safer fallback: mark the variable Protected and Masked, protect the branches/tags that deploy, scope it to its environment, and verify a non-protected branch pipeline cannot read it.
